# CARD DECK: CD-<RUN_ID>

**Run:** <RUN_ID>
**Project:** <PROJECT>
**Generated:** <YYYY-MM-DD>
**Generated by:** BECCA (RECON phase)

---

## DECK CONTRACT

```
If it isn't on a card, it didn't happen.
If it didn't produce a receipt, it isn't accepted.

Every agent executes by CARD_ID. No freeform work.
Every output includes a CARD_RECEIPT. No receipt = INVALID_OUTPUT.
Every card has acceptance criteria. Not met = not done.
```

---

## CORE CARDS (Required on EVERY run — "you shall not pass" set)

| Card ID | Title | Required By | Fail Mode |
|---------|-------|-------------|-----------|
| CARD-CORE-001 | Load Policy Pack + Pheromones | ALL agents | FAIL_BLOCKING — no work without context |
| CARD-CORE-002 | Backup-First Proof | Ant (if data ops) | FAIL_BLOCKING — no patch without backup |
| CARD-CORE-003 | Scope Lock | Ant | FAIL_BLOCKING — no patch without scope |
| CARD-CORE-004 | Evidence Capture Plan | Ant | FAIL_BLOCKING — no report without evidence plan |
| CARD-CORE-005 | Post-Change Verification | Ant | FAIL_BLOCKING — no handoff without verification |

**Rule:** If ANY CORE card is missing from `cards_executed`, Ghost returns `FAIL_BLOCKING`.

---

## TASK CARDS (Per-task — generated by BECCA per task packet)

| Card ID | Title | Phase | Acceptance Criteria |
|---------|-------|-------|-------------------|
| CARD-TASK-001 | <task-specific card> | DISCOVERY | <criteria> |
| CARD-TASK-002 | <task-specific card> | FOOTPRINT | <criteria> |
| CARD-TASK-003 | <task-specific card> | PATCH | <criteria> |

*(BECCA generates task cards from the task packet objective and target files)*

---

## TOOL CARDS (How to run tests, build, deploy — project-specific)

| Card ID | Title | When Used | Acceptance Criteria |
|---------|-------|-----------|-------------------|
| CARD-TOOL-001 | Run Tests | VERIFY phase | All tests pass, output captured |
| CARD-TOOL-002 | Build Check | VERIFY phase | Build succeeds, no errors |
| CARD-TOOL-003 | Deploy | Post-Ghost approval | Deploy command succeeds, smoke test passes |

*(BECCA populates from project OPERATOR_MANUAL commands section)*

---

## CARD STRUCTURE (every card must follow this format)

```markdown
# <CARD_ID>: <TITLE>

**Purpose:** <1 sentence — why this card exists>
**Phase:** <DISCOVERY / FOOTPRINT / BACKUP / PATCH / VERIFY / REPORT>
**Required:** YES / CONDITIONAL (<condition>)

## INPUTS REQUIRED
- <input 1 — what the agent needs before starting>
- <input 2>

## STEPS (max 5)
1. <action — specific, verifiable>
2. <action>
3. <action>

## OUTPUT REQUIRED
- <artifact this card must produce>

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
| # | Check | Pass/Fail |
|---|-------|-----------|
| 1 | <specific, testable condition> | |
| 2 | <specific, testable condition> | |

## FAIL MODE
<what to do if acceptance criteria not met — e.g., STOP, retry, escalate>

## MANUAL REFERENCES (per step)
Cards point to the exact manual sections needed — no one reads the whole manual for one action.
- Step 1: `<file.md>` Section <N>: <description>
- Step 2: `<file.md>` Section <N>: <description>
- etc.
```

---

## CARD_RECEIPT FORMAT (mandatory in every agent output)

```markdown
## CARD RECEIPT

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Deck ID | CD-<RUN_ID> |
| Policy Pack | PP-<YYYY-MM-DD> |
| Cards Executed | CARD-CORE-001, CARD-CORE-003, CARD-TASK-001 |
| Cards Skipped | CARD-CORE-002 (WAIVER: no data ops) |
| Blockers | NONE |

### Card Outputs

| Card ID | Output | Acceptance Met? |
|---------|--------|----------------|
| CARD-CORE-001 | Policy loaded, 3 pheromones noted | YES |
| CARD-CORE-003 | Scope: 2 files, CODE_ONLY | YES |
| CARD-TASK-001 | Discovery findings attached | YES |
```

**Hard rule:** If CARD_RECEIPT is missing from any agent output → `OUTPUT_INVALID: CARD_COMPLIANCE_FAILED`.

---

## CARD_WAIVER FORMAT (for controlled skipping)

```markdown
### CARD WAIVER

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Card ID | CARD-CORE-002 |
| Reason | All operations are CODE_ONLY — no data ops present |
| Risk | LOW |
| Mitigation | Data Op Classification table shows all CODE_ONLY |
| Approved By | Ghost Inspector (or Operator for CORE waivers) |
```

**Rule:** No waiver → no skip. Skipping without waiver triggers NUCLEAR (V-13 equivalent).

---

## SUB-DECK / NESTED INDEX RULE

A Master Card can point to a Sub-Deck — a nested set of cards with its own `deck_id`.

```
Master Card: CARD-TASK-003 (Complex Migration)
  └─ Sub-Deck: SD-TASK-003
     ├─ CARD-SD-001: Export source data
     ├─ CARD-SD-002: Transform schema
     └─ CARD-SD-003: Import to target
```

### Sub-Deck Rules
1. Sub-Deck has its own `deck_id` prefixed with `SD-` (e.g., `SD-TASK-003`)
2. Sub-Deck cards follow the same CARD STRUCTURE (Purpose, Inputs, Steps, Output, Acceptance Criteria, Fail Mode)
3. The Master Card's acceptance criteria includes: "Sub-Deck SD-<ID> receipt present with all cards executed"
4. Sub-Deck produces its OWN CARD_RECEIPT (nested inside the Master Card's output)
5. Ghost validates BOTH receipts: the main deck AND every sub-deck
6. Sub-Decks cannot nest further (max depth = 1) — if you need deeper, split the task

### Sub-Deck Receipt Format
```markdown
### SUB-DECK RECEIPT: SD-<ID>

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Sub-Deck ID | SD-<ID> |
| Parent Card | CARD-TASK-<NNN> |
| Cards Executed | CARD-SD-001, CARD-SD-002, CARD-SD-003 |
| Cards Skipped | NONE |
| Blockers | NONE |
```

### When to Use Sub-Decks
- Task has 3+ sequential operations that each need their own acceptance criteria
- Complex migrations, multi-step data operations, multi-service deployments
- BECCA generates sub-decks when task complexity warrants (not for simple CODE_ONLY tasks)
